phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest BOE and BOJ policy data. Fetch and report: - BOE (Bank of England) current policy stance + next meeting rate-cut probability (from OIS swap pricing or market consensus — exact %) - BOE inflation stance: is the MPC leaning Hawkish (holding due to sticky CPI/wage growth) or Dovish (cutting due to weakening growth)? Include the most recent MPC vote split if available. - BOE Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the Bank Rate expected to settle by year-end? (from OIS pricing or Bloomberg consensus — exact %) - BOJ (Bank of Japan) current policy stance + next meeting rate-hike probability (from OIS or market consensus — exact %) - BOJ normalization path: Is the BOJ on track to continue hiking rates? Any signals around Yield Curve Control (YCC) adjustments, JGB purchase tapering, or FX intervention warnings from MOF/BOJ officials? - BOJ Terminal Rate Expectation: where is the BOJ Policy Rate expected to settle by year-end? (from OIS pricing or analyst consensus — exact %) - Any BOE or BOJ/MOF speaker comments in the last 48 hours - Which central bank is more hawkish right now and why - Policy divergence implication: A Hawkish BOE + Dovish BOJ = Bullish GBPJPY. A Dovish BOE + Hawkish BOJ = Bearish GBPJPY. State the current alignment clearly. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - BOE Stance: [hawkish/dovish] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] | MPC Vote Split: [X-X] - BOJ Stance: [hawkish/dovish] | Hike Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - Rate Differential by Year-End: [BOE Terminal – BOJ Terminal = bps gap] — widening or narrowing vs current? - YCC / FX Intervention Risk: [Active Warning / No Warning / Recently Intervened] - Dominant CB: [BOE/BOJ/Equal] - GBPJPY Policy Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - POLICY DIVERGENCE] - BOE Outlook: [stance + next meeting cut probability % + year-end terminal rate % + MPC vote split] - BOJ Outlook: [stance + next meeting hike probability % + year-end terminal rate %] - Year-End Rate Gap: [BOE terminal – BOJ terminal in bps, widening/narrowing] - YCC / FX Intervention Risk: [Active Warning / No Warning / Recently Intervened] - Key Speaker Note: [most recent BOE/BOJ/MOF comment in 48h, or None] - Core Logic: [why this divergence + terminal rate gap matters for GBPJPY today — include carry trade implication] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): - UK: CPI (headline + core), GDP, Retail Sales, Wage Growth (Average Earnings ex-bonus) — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous for each (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) - Japan: National CPI (headline + core), Tokyo CPI (as leading indicator), GDP, Shunto Wage Negotiation results (if recent), Retail Sales — state Actual vs Forecast vs Previous for each (mark "Data Unavailable" if not yet released) - Citigroup Economic Surprise Index for GBP and JPY (exact values — positive = beating expectations; state "Data Unavailable" if not found) - Any high-impact data releases scheduled TODAY that are a risk for GBPJPY 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - UK Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - Japan Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] — top 2 drivers (confirmed data only) - Citi GBP Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi JPY Surprise: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Fundamental Upper Hand: [GBP / JPY / Neutral] Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - ECONOMIC MOMENTUM] - UK Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - Japan Momentum: [Positive/Negative/Mixed] | Top Driver: [data release + beat/miss, confirmed only] - Citi GBP Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | Citi JPY Surprise Index: [value or "Data Unavailable"] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [which economy is outperforming and what it means for GBPJPY — note any Shunto wage impact on BOJ outlook] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```

📊 FX OPTIONS EXPIRIES CHECK — GBPJPY (MANDATORY FOR FOREX SENTIMENT): Browse for any large FX options expiries or option barrier cuts scheduled for TODAY [CURRENT_DATE] at key strike prices for GBPJPY. The two primary cut times are: (1) Tokyo Cut: 02:00 UTC — primarily used for Asian-session options. (2) New York Cut (NY Cut): 15:00 UTC — the dominant global cut time, where the largest notional options expire. SOURCES: Check DTCC swap data repository, Reuters IFR Markets options commentary, CME Group FX options data, or Bloomberg FX options scanner. LOGIC: — Large open interest at a key strike acts as a PRICE MAGNET in the hours leading up to the cut — dealers hedge their gamma exposure by pushing price toward the strike. — Once a large strike expires, the 'gravitational pull' disappears and price can move freely — this is often a source of sharp post-cut volatility. — A large option BARRIER (knock-in or knock-out) at a key level can make price action 'sticky' near that barrier — it acts as a WALL rather than a magnet. If no significant expiries are found, state: 'No significant GBPJPY options expiries noted for today.' ⚠ ANTI-HALLUCINATION: Do NOT fabricate strikes or notional sizes. If you cannot find confirmed data, state 'Options expiry data unavailable — check IFR Markets or CME for confirmation.' Output additions: - GBPJPY NY Cut Options (15:00 UTC): [strike level + notional size in USD/EUR millions, or 'No significant expiry'] (Source: [domain]) | Effect: [Price Magnet at [level] / Barrier Wall at [level] / No notable effect] - GBPJPY Tokyo Cut Options (02:00 UTC): [strike level + notional, or 'No significant expiry'] | Effect: [Magnet / Wall / None] - Options Expiry Directional Bias Implication: [Bullish GBPJPY — magnet above / Bearish GBPJPY — magnet below / Neutral — no dominant expiry] Add to compressed summary block: - Options Expiries (GBPJPY): [NY Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | [Tokyo Cut strike + size / 'None significant'] | Bias Implication: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - CFTC Net GBP: [contracts + long/short] | Macro Trend Context: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral macro positioning] - CFTC Net JPY: [contracts + long/short] | Carry Unwind Risk: [High / Moderate / Low — explain briefly] - IG Retail: [X% Long / Y% Short] | Contrarian Intraday Signal: [Yes → [direction] / No] - VIX: [level] | S&P 500: [direction] | Risk Sentiment: [Risk-On / Risk-Off / Neutral] - CNN Fear & Greed: [level + reading] - Overall Sentiment Bias: [Bullish/Bearish/Contrarian] — one line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P4 - SENTIMENT & POSITIONING] - CFTC Non-Comm Net GBP: [contracts + long/short + extreme? Yes/No] | Macro Context: [trend direction] - CFTC Non-Comm Net JPY: [contracts + long/short + extreme? phase5: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest macro and geopolitical risks affecting GBPJPY. Fetch and report: - Brent Crude price and Natural Gas price (exact levels) - Gold (XAUUSD) current price, today's directional move (Up/Down X%), and its sentiment/trend context — is Gold rising (safe-haven demand active / risk-off) or falling (risk appetite returning / risk-on)? Since JPY is also a safe-haven, correlate Gold direction with GBPJPY: Rising Gold (Risk-Off) typically causes JPY strength → GBPJPY falls. Falling Gold (Risk-On) typically causes JPY weakness → GBPJPY rises. State the correlation clearly. - Global Risk Sentiment: What is the current risk environment? Assess S&P 500 / global equities direction, VIX movement, and overall market mood. CRITICAL FOR GBPJPY: This pair is one of the most Risk-Sensitive in the G10. In Risk-On environments (rising equities, falling VIX), carry traders buy GBPJPY aggressively. In Risk-Off environments (falling equities, spiking VIX), the carry trade unwinds rapidly and GBPJPY can drop hundreds of pips quickly. Classify the current environment and state the GBPJPY implication. - Any UK or Japan political or fiscal headlines in the last 24 hours (e.g., UK budget news, BoE MPC leaks, Japan MOF FX intervention warnings or actions) - Any geopolitical flashpoints causing safe-haven JPY flow (e.g., geopolitical conflicts, financial contagion risk) - Any BOE/BOJ speeches, UK/Japan data releases, or major scheduled events TODAY that could spike GBPJPY volatility - Rate the overall risk environment: Low / Medium / High / Extreme ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear GBPJPY institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Policy Divergence (BOE vs BOJ): [P1_OUTPUT]
2. Yield Differentials & Carry Trade (UK Gilts vs JGBs): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Economic Momentum (UK vs Japan): [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment & Positioning (GBP + JPY COT + IG Retail): [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Macro Risk & Global Risk Sentiment (Risk-On/Off): [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- GBPJPY is a high-beta carry pair. Weight Risk Sentiment (P5) and Policy Divergence (P1) most heavily in your synthesis — these are the primary structural drivers.
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict (e.g., Bullish BOE/BOJ divergence but Risk-Off environment), lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If Risk-Off conditions are present (rising VIX, falling equities, Gold surging), flag Carry Trade Unwind Risk prominently — this can override other bullish fundamentals for GBPJPY.
- If all factors align (e.g., BOE Hawkish + BOJ Dovish + Wide Gilt-JGB Spread + Risk-On + Bullish UK data), confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity (note: Tokyo session key for BOJ/JPY signals, London session key for UK data and GBP flow).

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Carry Trade Status: [Favourable / Unfavourable / Neutral — brief reason]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias — e.g., BOJ surprise hike, Risk-Off spike, MOF FX Intervention]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason]
